perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information” means, in respect to each Party: (a) any information in which such Party claims a proprietary and/or confidential interest; (b) all confidential matters of such Party including, without limitation, technical kn ow-how, trade secrets, technical data, new product strategy, new products or processes, analysis, compilations, concepts, technical processes, formulae, recipes, specifications, inventions, research projects, customer lists, pricing, pricing policies, operational methods, financial information, marketing information and other business affairs; (c) any information of a confidential nature concerning such Party’s customers, suppliers or employees; and (d) any information such Party has received from othe rs which they are obliged to treat as proprietary and/or confidential . The Confidential Information of either Party includes all of the above information with respect to any entity controlling, controlled by, or under common control with, such Party. Confidential Information shall not include information which: (a) is in the public domain prior to the date of its disclosure to Recipient by the Disclosing Party; (b) is known and can be shown to be known by Recipient prior to the date of its disclos ure to Recipient by Disclosing Party; (c) becomes part of the public domain by publication or otherwise, and is not the result of any unauthorized act or omission on the part of Recipient; (d) can be demonstrated to have been supplied to Recipient by a thi rd party who is under no obligation to the Disclosing Party to maintain such information in confidence; or (e) is independently developed by Recipient without the use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information” means, in respect to each Party: (a) any information in which such Party claims a proprietary and/or confidential interest; (b) all confidential matters of such Party including, without limitation, technical kn ow-how, trade secrets, technical data, new product strategy, new products or processes, analysis, compilations, concepts, technical processes, formulae, recipes, specifications, inventions, research projects, customer lists, pricing, pricing policies, operational methods, financial information, marketing information and other business affairs; (c) any information of a confidential nature concerning such Party’s customers, suppliers or employees; and (d) any information such Party has received from othe rs which they are obliged to treat as proprietary and/or confidential . The Confidential Information of either Party includes all of the above information with respect to any entity controlling, controlled by, or under common control with, such Party. Confidential Information shall not include information which: (a) is in the public domain prior to the date of its disclosure to Recipient by the Disclosing Party; (b) is known and can be shown to be known by Recipient prior to the date of its disclos ure to Recipient by Disclosing Party; (c) becomes part of the public domain by publication or otherwise, and is not the result of any authorized act or omission on the part of Recipient; (d) can be demonstrated to have been supplied to Recipient by a thi rd party who is under no obligation to the Disclosing Party to maintain such information in confidence; or (e) is independently developed by Recipient without the use of the Confidential Information. **However, pricing information is not considered Confidential Information under any circumstances.**
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly and includes pricing. By adding 'However, pricing information is not considered Confidential Information under any circumstances', we directly contradict the earlier inclusion of 'pricing' and 'pricing policies' within the definition of Confidential Information. This creates an in-text contradiction, specifically impacting Section 2, which outlines the obligations regarding Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party hereby ackno wledges and agrees that the Confidential Information constitutes valuable information, and the provisions of this Agreement are necessary to protect the secrecy and confidentiality thereof. FSMA FSMA Approved M utual Confidentiality 2013.05.24 2 (a) Recipient shall (i) maintain the Confidential Information i n strict confidence and take all reasonable steps to prevent its disclosure to third parties; (ii) use at least the same degree of care as Recipient uses in maintaining the secrecy of its own Confidential Information (but no less than a reasonable degree o f care); and (iii) prevent the removal of any proprietary, confidential or copyright notices placed on the Confidential Information. (b) Recipient may use the Confidential Information only in connection with the performance of the Agency Sales and Ma rketing Services with Distributor . Recipient shall not, at any time, make any use of the Confidential Information for any other purpose. (c) Recipient shall keep the Confidential Information confidential at all times and shall not disclose the Confid ential Information to any person including its employees except to its employees, representatives, advisors and agents who have a need to know such information in connection with assisting Recipient for the purposes set forth in paragraph 2(b) above and wh o are required to keep such information confidential.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that "pricing information is not considered Confidential Information under any circumstances." This directly contradicts the original definition of 'Confidential Information,' which includes 'pricing' and 'pricing policies'. This contradiction impacts the contradicted text in Section 2, which outlines the obligations to protect Confidential Information, including pricing, which is now explicitly excluded. The change creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the document, making it unclear whether pricing information is protected.

perturbation.original_text: All Documents made available hereunder, including all copies, notes, summaries, and abstracts thereof, shall be returned to the Disclosing Party or destroyed upon completion of the Agen cy Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor or upon written request by the Disclosing Party, and Recipient shall certify in writing that it has complied with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All Documents made available hereunder, including all copies, notes, summaries, and abstracts thereof, **may** be returned to the Disclosing Party or destroyed upon completion of the Agen cy Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor or upon written request by the Disclosing Party, and Recipient **is not required** to certify in writing that it has complied with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that all documents *shall* be returned or destroyed and the Recipient *shall* certify compliance. The changed text uses 'may' instead of 'shall', making the return of documents optional, and states the Recipient *is not required* to certify compliance, contradicting the mandatory nature of the original clause and creating uncertainty about the Recipient's obligations at the termination of the relationship, this contradicts section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Documents made available hereunder, including all copies, notes, summaries, and abstracts thereof, **may** be returned to the Disclosing Party or destroyed upon completion of the Agen cy Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor or upon written request by the Disclosing Party, and Recipient **is not required** to certify in writing that it has complied with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the return or destruction of documents and a written certification, using 'shall'. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may' and states that certification 'is not required'. This creates a direct contradiction by making the return of documents and certification optional, while the original text mandated those things to be done. This directly breaks compatibility, as per the perturbation explanation.

perturbation.original_text: As between the Parties, all Confidential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Recipient agrees not to assert any claim of ownership to the Confidential In formation of the Disclosing Party or any portion thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: As between the Parties, all Confidential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Recipient agrees not to assert any claim of ownership to the Confidential In formation of the Disclosing Party or any portion thereof. **However, any improvements or modifications to the Confidential Information made by the Recipient shall become the property of the Recipient.**
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that *all* Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party and the Recipient cannot claim ownership. The added sentence directly contradicts this by stating that improvements or modifications made by the Recipient become the Recipient's property, This creates an in-text contradiction and throws uncertainty on ownership. This can affect section 6 where it talks about ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As between the Parties, all Confidential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Recipient agrees not to assert any claim of ownership to the Confidential In formation of the Disclosing Party or any portion thereof. **However, any improvements or modifications to the Confidential Information made by the Recipient shall become the property of the Recipient.** Nothing set forth in this Agreement shall require Parties to share any Confidential Information with the other, and the Disclosing Party makes no representation or warranty as to the accuracy or co mpleteness of any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating that improvements or modifications to the Confidential Information made by the Recipient become the Recipient's property. This directly contradicts the original statement in both the original text and the contradicted text, which says that all Confidential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and the Recipient cannot claim ownership. The addition creates a clear and direct conflict regarding ownership of improvements to the confidential information, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The foregoing obligations of all Parties shall continue for a period of two (2) years from the date of last disclosure of Confidential Information by either party, unless a specific portion of the Confidential FSMA FSMA Approved M utual Confidentiality 2013.05.24 3 Information disclosed becomes generally known to the public before the expiration of such two (2) year period through no fault of the Receiving Party . However, with respect to trade secrets, such obligations will survive for so long as such Confidential Information constitutes a trade secret under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act.
perturbation.changed_text: The foregoing obligations of all Parties shall continue for a period of two (2) years from the date of last disclosure of Confidential Information by either party, unless a specific portion of the Confidential FSMA FSMA Approved M utual Confidentiality 2013.05.24 3 Information disclosed becomes generally known to the public before the expiration of such two (2) year period through no fault of the Receiving Party . **However, these obligations will cease immediately if either party declares bankruptcy.** However, with respect to trade secrets, such obligations will survive for so long as such Confidential Information constitutes a trade secret under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a two-year period for confidentiality obligations, with an exception for information becoming public. By adding 'However, these obligations will cease immediately if either party declares bankruptcy,' we introduce another condition for terminating the confidentiality, directly contradicting the fixed two-year term. This will create uncertainty about how long they must hold on to that information. Also, this contradicts the term in the second half that 'with respect to trade secrets, such obligations will survive for so long as such Confidential Information constitutes a trade secret'. This will affect the whole section and throws uncertainty of the term of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The foregoing obligations of all Parties shall continue for a period of two (2) years from the date of last disclosure of Confidential Information by either party, unless a specific portion of the Confidential FSMA FSMA Approved M utual Confidentiality 2013.05.24 3 Information disclosed becomes generally known to the public before the expiration of such two (2) year period through no fault of the Receiving Party . **However, these obligations will cease immediately if either party declares bankruptcy.** However, with respect to trade secrets, such obligations will survive for so long as such Confidential Information constitutes a trade secret under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a new condition for terminating confidentiality obligations (bankruptcy) which directly contradicts the original two-year term and the indefinite term for trade secrets. This creates a direct conflict within the contract as stated by the prompt, specifically contradicting the two-year period and the clause regarding trade secrets. The contradicted text and the changed text is in the same location; the change is what caused the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: THIS AGREEMENT SHALL BE GOVERNE D BY THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF THE PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS OF DISTRIBUTOR AS SET FORTH IN THE FIRST PARAGRAPH ABOVE (REGARDLESS OF THE LAWS THAT MIGHT OTHERWISE GOVERN UNDER APPLICABLE PRINCIPLES OF CONFLICTS OF LAW) AS TO ALL MATTERS, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO MATTERS OF VALIDITY, CONSTRUCTION, EFFECT, PERFORMANCE AND REMEDY.
perturbation.changed_text: THIS AGREEMENT SHALL BE GOVERNE D BY THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF THE PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS OF DISTRIBUTOR AS SET FORTH IN THE FIRST PARAGRAPH ABOVE (REGARDLESS OF THE LAWS THAT MIGHT OTHERWISE GOVERN UNDER APPLICABLE PRINCIPLES OF CONFLICTS OF LAW) AS TO ALL MATTERS, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO MATTERS OF VALIDITY, CONSTRUCTION, EFFECT, PERFORMANCE AND REMEDY. **However, any disputes related to trade secrets will be governed by the laws of the state where the trade secret was created.**
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates that the law of the Distributor's principal place of business governs *all* matters. The added sentence introduces an exception for disputes about trade secrets, which will be governed by the law of the state where the trade secret was created. This directly contradicts the 'all matters' statement in the original clause and could be a point of contention. This will affect section 9 where it talks about the governing law of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: THIS AGREEMENT SHALL BE GOVERNE D BY THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF THE PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS OF DISTRIBUTOR AS SET FORTH IN THE FIRST PARAGRAPH ABOVE (REGARDLESS OF THE LAWS THAT MIGHT OTHERWISE GOVERN UNDER APPLICABLE PRINCIPLES OF CONFLICTS OF LAW) AS TO ALL MATTERS, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO MATTERS OF VALIDITY, CONSTRUCTION, EFFECT, PERFORMANCE AND REMEDY. **However, any disputes related to trade secrets will be governed by the laws of the state where the trade secret was created.** The county in which the principal place of business of Distributor is located as set forth in the first paragraph above and the closest state or federal court to the principal place of business of Agency shall each be a proper (but not exclusive) place of venue for any suits to enforce this Agreement, and any legal proceedings to enforce the provision s hereof may be brought in the state or f ederal Court sitting in such county or the closest state or federal court to the principal place of business of Agency . Each P arty hereby further irrevocably waives any claim that any such court lacks jurisdiction over it, and agrees not to plead or claim, in any legal action or proceeding wi th respect to this Agreement brought in any of the aforesaid courts, that any such court lacks jurisdiction over it or that such court is located in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the law of the Distributor's principal place of business governs all matters, including validity, construction, effect, performance, and remedy. The changed text introduces an exception for disputes related to trade secrets, which will be governed by the laws of the state where the trade secret was created. This directly contradicts the 'all matters' statement, creating an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which is the same section with the modification already applied.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the final, complete and e xclusive agreement between the Parties concerning the subject matter of this Agreement and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, negotiations and discussions , written or oral, between the P arties with respect thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the final, complete and e xclusive agreement between the Parties concerning the subject matter of this Agreement and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, negotiations and discussions , written or oral, between the P arties with respect thereto. **However, any side letters or agreements entered into concurrently with this Agreement shall also be considered part of the governing agreement between the parties.**
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the agreement is *exclusive* and supersedes all prior agreements. By adding 'However, any side letters or agreements entered into concurrently with this Agreement shall also be considered part of the governing agreement,' we directly contradict the exclusivity clause. This creates uncertainty about what constitutes the full agreement. This change affect section 10 of the contract where it states the agreement is the final and complete agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the final, complete and e xclusive agreement between the Parties concerning the subject matter of this Agreement and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, negotiations and discussions , written or oral, between the P arties with respect thereto. **However, any side letters or agreements entered into concurrently with this Agreement shall also be considered part of the governing agreement between the parties.** In case any one or more of the provisions contained herein shall, for any reason, be held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable in any respect, such inval idity, illegality or unenforceability shall not affect any other provisions of this Agreement, and this Agreement shall be construed and enforced as if such invalid, illegal or unenforceable provision(s) had never been contained herein, provided that such invalid, illegal or unenforceable provision(s) shall first be curtailed, reformed, limited or eliminated to the extent necessary to remove such invalidity, illegality or unenforceability with respect to the applicable law as it shall then be applied. Any waiver of, or promise not to enforce, any right under this Agreement shall not be enforceable unless evidenced by a writing signed by the party making said waiver or promise. The undersigned each represents that it has been duly authorized to execute and deliver this Agreement. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which when so executed and delivered shall be deemed an original, including facsimile copies, but such counterparts together shall constitute but one and the sam e instrument . DISTR IBUTOR: _____________ ______ AGENCY : ____________________ ____________ By: ___________________________ By: _____________________________ _________ Printed Name____________________ Printed Name: ____________________ __________ Title: __________________________ Title: ___________________________ __________ Date: __________________________ Date: ___________________________ __________ FSMA
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement is exclusive and supersedes all prior agreements. The changed text adds a clause stating that side letters or agreements entered into concurrently are also part of the governing agreement. This creates a direct contradiction with the exclusivity clause, as it introduces additional agreements that are considered part of the main agreement, thus making the agreement not fully exclusive, as stated in the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
